President Donald Trump endorsed U.S. Rep. Andy Barr for Senate and Ralph Alvarado for a House seat in Kentucky. Trump called Barr a 'proven political winner' ahead of crowded primaries.

Donald Trump argued on appeal that the First Amendment protected his incitement of the January 6 mob, while simultaneously claiming the First Amendment does not protect a comedian's right to insult him. This came after an armed man attempted to enter the Correspondents Dinner.

President Donald Trump faces a critical deadline as the 60-day mark approaches since the start of the Iran conflict, with the War Powers Resolution requiring troop withdrawal unless Congress extends the deadline. More Republicans are breaking ranks and threatening to support measures to force an end to the fighting.

Hundreds of thousands of people protested in dozens of US cities for International Workers' Day, commemorating the 1886 Chicago struggle for an eight-hour workday.

Birden fazla haber, Başkan Donald Trump'ın son dönemdeki yalan ifadelerini ve zihinsel keskinliğiyle ilgili iddialarını öne çıkarıyor. Eski bir ordu binbaşısı ve muhafazakar bir avukat da dahil olmak üzere eleştirmenler, Trump'ın söylemini ve yasal girişimlerini kınadı. Trump, bilişsel bir testi geçtiğiyle övünerek birçok destekçisinin bunu yapamayacağını ima etti.
Multiple reports highlight President Donald Trump's recent false statements and claims about his mental acuity. Critics, including a former Army major and a conservative attorney, have denounced Trump's rhetoric and legal actions. Trump boasted about passing a cognitive test, suggesting many of his supporters could not.
Dünya çapında 1 Mayıs mitinglerinde işçi hakları ve barış talepleri
May Day rallies worldwide demand workers' rights and peace
3 src · 8 it2026-05-02 00:01
BreakThrough News · BreakThrough News · BreakThrough News · BreakThrough News · People's Dispatch · BreakThrough News · BreakThrough News · Brasil de Fato
▸ sentez / synthesis
1 Mayıs gösterileri küresel çapta gerçekleşti; Avrupa'da yüz binlerce, ABD'de binlerce kişi sosyal adalet, barış ve işçi hakları talep etti. Chicago'da UAW başkanı Shawn Fain ve bir Donanma gazisi savaşa ve milyarder açgözlülüğüne karşı konuştu. San Francisco'da protestocular ICE'in kaldırılmasını ve adil ücret talep etti. Madison'da okullar göçmenlerle dayanışma için kapandı. Brezilya'da işçiler 6x1 çalışma düzeninin sona ermesine odaklandı.
May Day demonstrations took place globally, with hundreds of thousands in Europe and thousands in the US calling for social justice, peace, and workers' rights. In Chicago, UAW president Shawn Fain and a Navy veteran spoke against war and billionaire greed. In San Francisco, protesters demanded ICE removal and fair wages. In Madison, schools closed in solidarity with immigrants. In Brazil, workers focused on ending the 6x1 work schedule.

President Trump signed a permit for the Bridger Pipeline Expansion, dubbed 'Keystone Light,' which would transport Canadian tar sands oil along a portion of the canceled Keystone XL route. Environmental groups criticized the decision.

Trade unions and left parties across South Asia held large-scale rallies and meetings for May Day. In Los Angeles, over 10,000 people marched for May Day and the 20th anniversary of 'A Day Without Immigrants.'

President Donald Trump informed Congress that hostilities with Iran have ended, despite a US naval blockade considered an act of war. Trump rejected an Iranian peace proposal. A poll shows 61% of Americans view the Iran war as a mistake.

Chicago Belediye Başkanı Brandon Johnson uluslararası dayanışma ve genel grev çağrısı yaptı. UAW Başkanı Shawn Fain işçileri 1 Mayıs'ı bir eylem günü olarak canlandırmaya çağırdı.
On May Day, thousands of teachers in North Carolina walked out demanding school funding. In Washington D.C., thousands marched led by union leaders. Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson called for international solidarity and a general strike. UAW President Shawn Fain urged workers to revive May Day as a day of action.

Outgoing Massachusetts Teachers Association president Max Page reflects on a decade of rank-and-file reformers turning the union into a militant, member-led force through strikes and stronger contracts.

According to an Axios report cited by AlterNet, the U.S. is more insulated from President Trump's global oil supply crisis than other nations, but consumers still feel the impact. The situation is described as causing 'whiplash' and is expected to influence upcoming elections.

Politico'ya göre Cumhuriyetçiler 'cehennem haftası'nda kilit yasaları geçirdi ancak bu, partiyi daha da bölme pahasına oldu. Muhafazakar isyan büyürken hayal kırıklığına uğramış üyeler Temsilciler Meclisi Başkanı'nı kötülüyor.
According to Politico, Republicans have passed key legislation during 'hell week' but at the cost of further fracturing the party. Frustrated members are badmouthing the House speaker as a conservative rebellion ramps up.

Palestinians marked May Day with a focus on the devastation of the labor market and living standards due to years of war and genocide.

In France, a proposal to allow some businesses to remain open on May Day prompted unions to defend the tradition of keeping it as a day off for workers.

Republicans corrupted Supreme Court, analysis says

New York Times columnist Jamelle Bouie argues that the Supreme Court is corrupt following a decision that allegedly gutted the Voting Rights Act. He suggests that something must be done about the court's corruption.

DOJ indictment of SPLC draws criticism as campaign of intimidation

▸ sentez / synthesis
Common Cause ve Demokrat milletvekilleri, Adalet Bakanlığı'nın Güney Yoksulluk Hukuk Merkezi'ni (SPLC) suçlamasını eleştiriyor; bir ihbarcı, savcılara davayı aceleye getirmeleri emredildiğini iddia ediyor.
Common Cause and Democratic lawmakers criticize the DOJ's indictment of the Southern Poverty Law Center, with a whistleblower alleging prosecutors were ordered to rush the case.
ABD işçi hareketi, grevler ve okul bırakmalarıyla İşçi Bayramı'nı yeniden sahipleniyor
US labor movement reclaims International Workers' Day with strikes and walkouts
3 src · 4 it2026-05-01 13:54
The OC Portal · Liberation News · The OC Portal · Common Dreams
▸ sentez / synthesis
2026 Mayıs Günü'nde ABD işçi hareketi, grevler ve öğrenci boykotlarıyla İşçi Bayramı'nı kutladı. 80 yılı aşkın süredir en büyük tek günlük öğrenci grevine 100.000'den fazla öğrenci katıldı ve ülke genelinde işçiler ve aktivistler de eylemlere destek verdi. Eylemler, bayramın ABD işçi hareketindeki kökenlerini yeniden sahiplenmeyi amaçlıyordu.
On May Day 2026, the U.S. labor movement observed International Workers' Day with strikes and student walkouts. More than 100,000 students participated in the largest one-day student strike in over 80 years, joined by workers and activists nationwide. The actions aimed to reclaim the holiday's origins in the U.S. labor movement.
Trump yönetimi İran görüşmeleri konusunda iç muhalefet ve hukuki sorularla karşı karşıya
Trump administration faces internal dissent and legal questions over Iran talks
1 src · 3 it2026-05-01 13:51
AlterNet · AlterNet · AlterNet
▸ sentez / synthesis
Başkan Donald Trump'ın yönetimi, üst düzey müttefiklerle yapılan düzenlemelerin hukuki durumu ve iç muhalefet nedeniyle inceleme altında. Eski Beyaz Saray yetkilisi Miles Taylor, içeridekilerin 'konuşmaktan korktuğunu' söyledi. Bu arada CNN, küfürlü bir yayın patlamasının ardından Trump yanlısı yorumcu Scott Jennings'e platform sağladığı için eleştiriliyor.
President Donald Trump's administration faces scrutiny over the legal status of arrangements with top allies and internal dissent. Former White House official Miles Taylor said insiders are 'scared to speak up.' Meanwhile, CNN faces criticism for platforming pro-Trump pundit Scott Jennings after a profane on-air tirade.
2025'te CEO maaşları işçi maaşlarından 20 kat daha hızlı arttı
CEO pay surged 20 times faster than worker pay in 2025
1 src · 1 it2026-05-01 13:51
Common Dreams
▸ sentez / synthesis
Common Dreams'e göre, 2019 ile 2025 arasında küresel reel işçi ücretleri yüzde 12 düşerken, reel CEO maaşları yüzde 54 arttı. En az dört CEO 2025'te 100 milyon doların üzerinde kazanç elde etti; Broadcom CEO'su Hock Tan 205 milyon doların üzerinde kazançla başı çekti.
According to Common Dreams, global real worker pay fell 12 percent while real CEO pay surged 54 percent between 2019 and 2025. At least four CEOs earned over $100 million in 2025, with Broadcom CEO Hock Tan leading at over $205 million.

Researchers tracking US strikes report that after increases in 2022 and 2023, strike activity dropped significantly in 2024 and remained low in 2025.

Commentators argue that International Workers' Day reminds us that individuals are happiest when they stand together, countering the narrative of individual effort.

Historian says Trump's appeal lies in promise of vitality

▸ sentez / synthesis

Historian Jackson Lears argued in a New York Times interview that President Donald Trump's appeal to voters stems from a promise of 'vitality,' a distinctly American cultural concept. Lears offered this explanation amid ongoing debate about Trump's persuasive power.

Georgia Governor Brian Kemp has disrupted Republican expectations for a favorable midterm redistricting map. Georgia has become a swing state with two Democratic senators despite having a Republican governor.

Military analyst Robert D. Kaplan wrote that President Donald Trump's war in Iran has exposed the gradual decline of U.S. naval supremacy. Kaplan's analysis was published in a new piece on Friday.
Analiz: Trump'ın müttefikleri ona sırt çeviriyor, anketler zayıflık gösteriyor
Trump's allies turn against him as polls show weakness, analysis finds
1 src · 3 it2026-05-01 11:11
AlterNet · AlterNet · AlterNet
▸ sentez / synthesis
Birden fazla rapor, Donald Trump'ın en yakın müttefiklerinin giderek ona sırt çevirdiğini, anketlerin ise onun inandığından daha zayıf bir konumda olduğunu gösteriyor. ProPublica'nın analizi, Trump'ın 2020 seçim darbe girişimini durdurmaya yardımcı olan en az 75 kariyer yetkilisinin tasfiye edildiğini ve kurumsal güvencelerin zayıfladığını ortaya koydu.
Multiple reports indicate that Donald Trump's closest allies are increasingly turning against him, while polls show him in a weaker position than he believes. An analysis by ProPublica found that at least 75 career officials who helped stop Trump's 2020 election coup attempt have been purged, weakening institutional guardrails.

An opinion piece argues that the Supreme Court's supermajority, led by Justice Alito, is gaslighting the public by pretending racism no longer exists in a ruling on the Voting Rights Act.

CounterPunch'tan kişisel bir deneme, Austin Halk Kütüphanesi'nin çatı bahçesinde çalışırken ayrıcalık ve eşitsizlik üzerine düşünüyor. Yazar, ne zaman yeterli olduğunu bilmek üzerine kafa yoruyor.
A personal essay from CounterPunch reflects on privilege and disparity while working from the rooftop garden of the Austin Public Library. The author muses on knowing when you have enough.
İnsanlık alanımızda kalmak önceliğimiz olmalı
To stay in our human wheelhouse must be our priority
1 src · 1 it2026-05-01 05:50
CounterPunch
▸ sentez / synthesis
CounterPunch, fikirlerin bastırılarak değil, görmezden gelinerek ezilebileceğini savunan bir deneme yayımlıyor. Yazı, Ursula K. Le Guin'in romanından bir karakteri alıntılayarak bireysel zihni bastırmaya karşı uyarıyor.
CounterPunch publishes an essay arguing that ideas cannot be crushed by suppression but by ignoring them. The piece quotes a character from Ursula K. Le Guin's novel, warning against stifling the individual mind.
İmparatorluktan bağımsızlık ilan etmek, birbirimize bağımlılığımızı ilan etmektir
Declaring independence from empire means interdependence

CounterPunch argues that the world faces multiple crises rooted in a common cause, including a potential energy shock from the shutdown of the Hormuz Strait. The article calls for recognizing interdependence.

Stroke unto death: Max's last year, a year later

CounterPunch publishes a personal reflection on the first anniversary of the death of a person named Max. The essay describes a year of loss, grief, and rituals including Bonoboville gatherings and art therapy.

A report notes that after the Voting Rights Act of 1965, Black political representation increased from less than 2% of seats, but progress remains fragile.

CounterPunch reviews Chuck Klosterman's book 'Football', which argues that American professional football is doomed to decline like horse racing. The review discusses the premise that the sport will become marginal.

CounterPunch reports on a production of Handel's opera Giulio Cesare directed by David McVicar. The article notes McVicar was later knighted by Queen Elizabeth for his work at Glyndebourne.

CounterPunch argues that the greatest threat to America is not government overreach but speech suppression. The article claims that the nation's founding figures would be considered threats today.

CounterPunch reports that the re-election of Donald Trump has reignited a feud between progressive and centrist Democrats. Progressives urge the party to rally around universal health care and raising wages, arguing that economic populism is a winning issue.

CounterPunch published an article discussing Alexandre Dumas and his novel 'The Count of Monte Cristo' in the context of the Age of Revolution (1789-1848), as termed by historian E.J. Hobsbawm.

King Charles III is in the United States on a visit aimed at repairing the US-UK alliance, described as 'special.' The visit coincides with the 250th anniversary of American independence.

CounterPunch reports on a new report from the National Council for Occupational Safety and Health (National COSH) titled 'Dangerous Workplaces.' The report details how employees risk life and limb due to employers' disregard of workplace safety standards.

CounterPunch published an article discussing radical Palestinian women in the struggle for liberation, noting that almost three years into the conflict with US-provided weapons, Palestinian resistance continues.

Common Dreams reports that a right-wing Supreme Court dealt a devastating blow to the Voting Rights Act, ensuring communities of color will increasingly be denied a voice in their destiny.

AlterNet reports a study finding that MAGA true believers who push conspiracy theories could wind up paying a price in their dating lives, as such beliefs are off-putting to potential partners.

Senator Rick Scott struggles to explain filibuster stance shift

▸ sentez / synthesis
Senatör Rick Scott (R-Fla.), Demokratlar çoğunluktayken benzer hamlelere karşı çıkmasına rağmen Senato filibuster'ını kaldırmaya yönelik yeni desteğini açıklamakta zorlandı. Scott, Demokratların Başkan Donald Trump'ın desteklediği yasa tasarılarına oylamayı engellemesinden şikayet ettiğinde bu tartışma yaşandı.
Senator Rick Scott (R-Fla.) faced difficulty explaining his newfound support for eliminating the Senate filibuster, after previously opposing similar moves when Democrats held the majority. The exchange occurred when Scott complained about Democrats blocking votes on bills favored by President Donald Trump.

Economist Scott Lincicome stated that despite a Supreme Court ruling striking down President Trump's emergency order tariff, consumers are unlikely to receive refunds for costs incurred during the illegal tax period. The court left the question of refunds unresolved.

President Trump is targeting Indiana Republicans who did not support gerrymandering, but faces opposition from within his own party. State Senate leader Rodric Bray is the ultimate target, though he is not on the primary ballot.

BreakThrough News reported live from Chicago, where students, teachers, and workers mobilized as part of a total shutdown under the slogan 'No Work. No School. No Shopping.'

In Maryland, community opposition forced a pause on plans to convert a warehouse into an ICE detention center. The Trump administration had allocated $45 billion for such centers under the One Big Beautiful Bill.

Trump administration bulldozes ancient site for border wall

▸ sentez / synthesis

The Trump administration bulldozed a 1,000-year-old archaeological site to make room for a second border wall, despite DHS being in talks with the wildlife refuge to protect the site, according to a local archaeologist.

Military expert says Trump's 'firehose of lunacy' shows cognitive decline

Retired Naval War College professor Tom Nichols stated that President Trump's 'firehose of lunacy' indicates cognitive decline, and America will be in peril until this is called out.

Nigerien students and activists gathered in Niamey to demand the expulsion of French corporations from the Sahel and the complete abolition of the CFA franc. They called on the Alliance of Sahel States to accelerate a common currency.

Miles Taylor, former Homeland Security chief of staff under Trump, said the president's Iran war plans are 'unraveled' and too treacherous for reality, during an interview with MSNBC's Nicolle Wallace.

The Real News Network criticized The Atlantic for publishing five articles about Twitch streamer Hasan Piker in two weeks, while publishing zero stories about Hind Rajab, Israeli killings of medical workers, or journalists.
Yüksek Mahkeme'nin oy hakkı kararı eleştirilere yol açtı
Supreme Court voting rights ruling sparks criticism
2 src · 3 it2026-04-30 21:21
AlterNet · AlterNet · Truthout
▸ sentez / synthesis
ABD Yüksek Mahkemesi, ikinci bir çoğunluğu Siyah bölge oluşturmayı amaçlayan Louisiana'nın kongre haritasını iptal etti. Eleştirmenler kararın Oy Hakkı Yasası'nı zayıflattığını söylüyor. Analistler, kararın 2026 ara seçimlerinde Demokrat katılımını bastırabileceği uyarısında bulundu. Sivil haklar avukatları kararı 'ayrımcılık için serbest geçiş' olarak nitelendirdi.
The U.S. Supreme Court struck down Louisiana's congressional map designed to create a second majority-Black district, in a ruling that critics say weakens the Voting Rights Act. Analysts warn the decision could suppress Democratic turnout in the 2026 midterms. Civil rights lawyers called it a 'free pass to discriminate.'

▸ sentez / synthesis
Savunma Bakanı adayı Pete Hegseth'in kongre oturumlarının ikinci gününde Senatör Elizabeth Warren, askeri hisse senedi ticareti konusundaki iddia edilen çelişkiler nedeniyle Hegseth'i sorguladı. Oturum ayrıca Hegseth'i savaş suçlusu olarak nitelendiren bir protestocu tarafından kesintiye uğradı.
During his second day of congressional hearings, Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth was confronted by Senator Elizabeth Warren over alleged contradictions regarding military stock trading. The hearing was also interrupted by a protester calling Hegseth a war criminal.

AlterNet reports that the Republican Party is experiencing a record number of retirements and candidate dropouts, with columnist Charles P. Pierce arguing the reasons are obvious.

AlterNet reports that law professor Richard Hasen called Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito a coward for his opinion in Louisiana v. Callais, which weakens the Voting Rights Act.

Electronic Intifada · Jacobin
▸ sentez / synthesis
Electronic Intifada, AİPAC'ın Gazze'deki soykırımı finanse eden ve teşvik eden adayları desteklediğini, ana akım medyanın ise İsrail askeri cinayetleri yerine yorumcu Hasan Piker'a odaklandığını bildirdi. Jacobin, seçkin merkezcilerin ifadelerinin Piker'ın yorumlarından daha kınanması gerektiğini ancak Demokrat çevrelerde kabul gören İslamofobi nedeniyle sorgulanmadığını savundu.
The Electronic Intifada reported that AIPAC backs candidates who fund and promote what it calls the Gaza genocide, while mainstream media focus on commentator Hasan Piker rather than Israeli military killings. Jacobin argued that statements by elite centrists are more reprehensible than Piker's comments, but go unchallenged due to acceptable Islamophobia in Democratic circles.

A new poll found that a majority of Americans oppose President Trump's proposals to redesign Washington, D.C., including a plan to place his name on U.S. currency, which is deeply unpopular.

Privacy groups are pushing Big Tech companies to notify users about federal surveillance after details emerged of the Trump administration's secret use of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to spy on anonymous critics.

Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel launched a 'My Signature for the Homeland' petition drive as a show of unity against Trump's threats. BreakThrough News announced a documentary featuring Díaz-Canel's first extensive interview with a U.S. journalist, premiering May 14.

AlterNet reports that former GOP strategist Rick Wilson criticized President Trump for pushing vanity projects like a White House ballroom while voters are concerned about other issues.

Senator Ron Wyden is facing backlash from NSA supporters after pushing for disclosure of a secret court opinion involving the Trump administration's use of NSA data. Senator Tom Cotton warned there would be 'consequences.'

İngiltere Başbakanı Keir Starmer, Yahudi erkeklere yönelik bıçaklı saldırı ve antisemitizme yanıtı nedeniyle protestocular tarafından yuhalandı.
Israel seized a Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters near Greece, detaining 175 activists. The UN's top Palestine expert condemned the action as piracy. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was also heckled by protesters over a knife attack on Jewish men and his response to antisemitism.

The American Federation of Government Employees (AFGE) celebrated the end of the 76-day Department of Homeland Security shutdown, the longest in U.S. history, and urged passage of the Shutdown Fairness Act to protect federal workers.

Senator Ron Wyden proposed a short-term FISA extension in exchange for declassifying a secret court opinion revealing surveillance abuses. Meanwhile, Representative Jim Himes is reportedly whipping votes to support the surveillance bill despite public opposition.

President Donald Trump launched a double-barreled attack on a two-term Republican senator, calling him 'very disloyal' and urging voters to oust him in the upcoming primary. Trump criticized the senator's impeachment vote and opposition to his Surgeon General pick.

The House passed a warrantless mass surveillance bill with 42 Democrats joining Republicans. Representative Ilhan Omar warned that the bill lacks meaningful reforms to prevent abuses.

The Supreme Court struck down a Louisiana redistricting map, with Republicans celebrating and experts warning they may be overselling the victory. Fox News' chief political analyst Brit Hume issued a wake-up call to Trump's GOP. The court also effectively eliminated a key aspect of the Voting Rights Act.

The House voted to strip sections 10205, 10206, and 10207 from the Farm Bill, with 71 Republicans and 6 Democrats supporting the removal. Friends of the Earth applauded the move, saying it upholds accountability for the pesticide industry.

The U.S. House of Representatives passed its version of the Farm Bill by a vote of 224-200. The House voted 280-142 to remove Cancer Gag Act language, including Sections 10205-7, a victory for public health and environmental advocates.

Migrants at the GEO Group-run facility in Michigan are on hunger strike, demanding due process, edible food, and an end to sleep deprivation.

Israeli naval forces intercepted vessels of the Global Sumud Flotilla near Crete, about 500 nautical miles from Gaza. Around 175 activists have been reportedly detained.

A platform backed by Amazon's Jeff Bezos allows investors to buy shares in rental homes, effectively becoming landlords and potentially driving up rents.

New York Supreme Court Justice David Fried rejected a claim by US Rep. Mike Lawler (R-NY) to remove Democratic candidate Effie Phillips-Staley's petitions from the ballot. Lawler is the only Republican incumbent in New York whose district is considered competitive.

The Intercept reports that House Speaker Mike Johnson added a cryptocurrency-related provision unrelated to domestic spying to secure hard-right GOP members' support for a domestic spy law. The provision is unlikely to pass the Senate.

Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth dismissed testimony from US soldiers who said they were put in danger, calling troop deaths 'tragic' but a 'consequence of conflict.' Hegseth also pledged that the military shows 'no quarter' to enemies, which experts say constitutes a war crime.

A growing rat infestation is plaguing tent cities in Gaza. Majd Sukar, head of the Preventive Health Department in Gaza City, said, 'We are suffering from two wars: the war of bombs, and the war of rats.'

California may soon have over 300 data centers, and locals worry about threats to water supply. One proposed data center would require 750,000 gallons of water per day and occupy 17 football fields' worth of land.

New York Times columnist Jamelle Bouie argued that it would be dishonest to deny that President Donald Trump is responsible for the political climate that led to the attempted assassination at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, and that Trump is afraid of the world he has helped build.

▸ sentez / synthesis

The Supreme Court issued a ruling that critics say eviscerates the Voting Rights Act. Justice Kagan called the decision a 'toothless law' for voting rights. The ruling struck down a Louisiana congressional map with a second majority-Black district, opening the door to gerrymandering in red states.

Elections expert Nathaniel Rakich said that President Donald Trump wants to seize control of the 2026 midterm elections but does not know how to accomplish that, and that the laws would not necessarily hurt Democrats.

An Israeli strike killed 4-year-old Mila Zayyat and her pregnant mother Ruqaya Zidan in the southern Lebanese village of Tayr Debba. Residents held a funeral for the two on Wednesday.

San Francisco öğrencileri, 'iş yok, okul yok, alışveriş yok' eylem gününün bir parçası olarak ulusal iş bırakma çağrısı yapıyor.
Unions and community organizations are planning a nationwide 'economic blackout' on May Day against billionaires and authoritarianism. Chicago teachers are organizing to build 'organized power at every level.' San Francisco students are calling for national walkouts as part of a 'no work, no school, no shopping' day of action.

Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth appeared before Congress and struck a hostile posture toward oversight. However, his top general immediately contradicted his attack, according to CNN Pentagon reporter Haley Britzky.

CIA whistleblower John Kiriakou argues that the War on Terror normalized preemptive war and unchecked executive power, enabling Donald Trump's authoritarianism.

Trump yönetimindeki Adalet Bakanlığı eylemleri içeriden ve yargıçlardan eleştiri alıyor
DOJ actions under Trump draw criticism from insiders and judges
1 src · 4 it2026-04-29 02:46
AlterNet · AlterNet · AlterNet · AlterNet
▸ sentez / synthesis
Başkan Donald Trump yönetimindeki Adalet Bakanlığı çeşitli eylemleri nedeniyle eleştirilerle karşılaştı. Federal bir yargıç, Arizona seçmen kayıtlarına erişim talep eden bir Adalet Bakanlığı davasını reddetti. Eski Adalet Bakanlığı yetkilileri, bir iddianamenin hayatları boyunca gördükleri en kötü dava olduğunu söyledi. Bir hukuki özet, çocuksu olarak eleştirildi. Kaynaklar, geçici Başsavcı Todd Blanche'ın James Comey'i suçlamadan önce onun için bir 'teşhir yürüyüşü' planladığını belirtti.
The Department of Justice under President Donald Trump has faced criticism over various actions. A federal judge dismissed a DOJ lawsuit seeking access to Arizona voter records. Former DOJ officials described one indictment as the worst case filed in their lifetime. A legal brief was criticized as childlike. Sources said acting AG Todd Blanche planned a 'perp-walk' for James Comey before charging him.

President Trump's military action in Iran is historically unpopular, even among his own base, according to a scholar cited by AlterNet. Republicans are said to be trapped in the conflict despite wanting out.

Former FBI agent Michael Feinberg and a former CIA director criticized interim AG Todd Blanche's indictment of former CIA head James Comey, which allegedly accused Comey of threatening Trump's life with seashells. Feinberg called it a mournful day for the country.

Under the War Powers Resolution, President Trump faces a May 1 deadline to report to Congress on the Iran conflict. He can seek a 30-day extension if he shows the war is an 'unavoidable military necessity.'

ABD ve Filipinler, çok sayıda ülkeden 17.000 askerin katılımıyla şimdiye kadarki en büyük Balikatan askeri tatbikatına başladı. Amerikan füze sistemleri, Tayvan'ın yaklaşık 160 kilometre güneyindeki Luzon Boğazı'na konuşlandırıldı.
The United States and the Philippines began their largest-ever Balikatan military exercises, involving 17,000 troops from multiple countries. American missile systems were deployed to the Luzon Strait, about 100 miles south of Taiwan.

Truthout reports that the CDC's latest vaccine recommendations for hepatitis B are not based on science, according to critics. Delays in the shot schedule could lead to hundreds of infant infections.

The Grayzone reports that a data analyst at Intuit, the company behind TurboTax, appeared on a company Zoom call in full IDF uniform. The article was originally published by Do Not Panic!

BreakThrough News reports that hundreds of educators in North Carolina are calling out on May 1 to join a 'no work, no school, no shopping' national shutdown, demanding more funding. Carson Bridges, president of Asheville City Association of Educators, spoke on the crisis.

Truthout reports that far-right Israeli settlers are increasingly crossing the border into Syria with at least tacit support from the Israeli military, as part of a push for 'Greater Israel'.

DropSite News reports that Apollo Global Management bankrupted two of Maine's largest paper mills, eliminating over 1,000 jobs. Now the CEO is funding a PAC to keep Senator Susan Collins in office.

In Australia, workers under 21 are paid less for the same jobs. New laws have entrenched this pay disparity, which critics call an injustice.

Truthout reports that an inmate says he will choose death over treatment unless New York changes its parole laws, as the disease threatening his existence may be the only thing that can set him free.

Experts argue that gig worker protections should not depend on each administration's priorities. They call for strong state classification laws and employer-of-record systems to provide stability.

Since 2024, a rift has emerged in the Democratic Party over whether to coordinate with billionaire-backed political networks to match Republicans. The clash between populists and party elites is now public.

For nearly 20 years, Pennsylvania funneled over $2.5 billion into private schools via tax credits for donors like Trump megadonor Jeffrey Yass, who received 90% of their contributions back as credits.

Common Dreams reports that a Public Citizen report details how President Trump and his sons have about $1 billion tied to crypto venture World Liberty Financial, which involves Binance.

Analiz, Trump'ın hasar kontrolünün ters teptiğini ve bir hesaplaşma gününün yaklaştığını öne sürüyor.
Major economists including Paul Krugman and Justin Wolfers warn that prolonged US-Iran conflict under President Trump will hit American consumers through higher oil prices. The analysis suggests a day of reckoning is coming as Trump's damage control backfires.

Commentators argue that the US is experiencing an unprecedented shift of domestic investments to the military-industrial complex, comparable only to the World War II buildup.

A recent shooting has been compared to the 1981 assassination attempt on Ronald Reagan, which led to the Brady Bill and background checks. The outcome of this event remains uncertain.

Critics highlight an Israeli law that criminalizes speech minimizing October 7 events or challenging the government's account, with penalties including up to 5 years for saying 'from the river to the sea'.

An author recounts how his father was arrested and tortured for dissident activities in communist Prague, and his mother made small concessions to appease authorities, reflecting on societal tolerance of evil.

President Donald Trump reportedly wants to use the assassination attempt against him to push for construction of a White House ballroom. However, Senate Majority Leader John Thune was noncommittal about whether Congress could approve the project, according to AlterNet.

A Wall Street Journal conservative columnist, William McGurn, warned Republicans against blaming the Secret Service for an apparent assassination attempt on President Trump. He criticized those calling the security handling an 'unmitigated failure,' as reported by AlterNet.

▸ sentez / synthesis

AlterNet'in haberine göre uyarı, Trump'ın Todd Blanche'ı geçici başsavcı ve Keith Sonderling'i geçici çalışma bakanı olarak atama kararlarıyla ilgili.
A Libertarian scholar warned that President Donald Trump risks violating the Constitution through his Cabinet picks, though he currently appears to be on the right side of the law. The warning concerns Trump's decisions to keep Todd Blanche as acting attorney general and Keith Sonderling as acting labor secretary, as reported by AlterNet.

AlterNet'in haberine göre tartışma, Deniz Kuvvetleri Sekreteri John Phelan'ın görevden alınmasının ardından geldi ve olası nedenlerden biri Trump'ın iki yeni savaş gemisi talebini yerine getirmedeki zorluklar.
A defense expert commented that rumors about Trump-class battleships prioritize President Donald Trump's 'insatiable ego.' The controversy follows the firing of Naval Secretary John Phelan, with one possible reason being difficulties in delivering on Trump's demand for two new battleships, as reported by AlterNet.

AlterNet'in haberine göre, Trump'ın yönetimi için Cumhuriyetçilerin yalnızca kendilerini suçlaması gerektiğini ve bunu Jül Sezar'a benzettiğini savunuyor.
Conservative legal scholar Gregg Nunziata wrote in The Atlantic that Donald Trump's unhinged leadership is the result of a decades-long plot by the right that has backfired. He argues that Republicans only have themselves to blame for Trump's rule, which he compares to Julius Caesar, as reported by AlterNet.

Pope Leo, when asked about immigration, accused the West of treating immigrants 'worse than animals' and questioned what the Global North is doing to help the Global South. The statement was reported by BreakThrough News.

The town of Deir Al-Balah in Gaza held its first local election since 2005. The vote was praised as a step forward, but occurred amid ongoing conflict and a law restricting candidate eligibility.

President Donald Trump survived a third assassination attempt, with a shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. Millions of Americans suspect the event was staged, while analysts note the suspect's extreme response to Trump's politics.

The builder of a ballroom for President Trump received $17 million for a project that the Biden administration estimated would cost $3 million. The original cost estimator said the estimate was inflated.

Over half of planned data center projects for 2026 have been delayed or cancelled due to organized community pushback across the US. Residents are blocking approvals and passing referendums.

The US Supreme Court upheld a gerrymandered Texas congressional map that is expected to give Republicans up to five additional seats in Congress, sparking controversy ahead of midterms.

The Ramones' self-titled debut album celebrates its 50th anniversary. The record is seen as speaking for those forgotten by the system, serving as a blueprint for later political punk.

New Mexico has become the latest state to end local and state cooperation with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Activists say this is a key way to obstruct Trump's mass deportation plans.

PAC'ler, zengin bağışçılar ve danışmanlardan oluşan yeni bir etki ağı, seçim finansmanı yasalarını kullanarak Demokrat kampanyalara milyonlar akıtıyor. Amaç, ılımlı adayları seçtirmek.
A new influence network of PACs, rich donors, and consultants is exploiting campaign finance law to pour millions into Democratic campaigns. The aim is to elect moderate candidates.

Drop Site News reports that April 27, 2026 was the deadliest day in Lebanon since the ceasefire, alongside a militant surge in Mali and the US Supreme Court weighing a Roundup case.

Working-class voters are having second thoughts about MAGA but are still leaving the Democratic Party. The party's reliance on college-educated suburbanites is seen as insufficient and unsustainable.

The fate of Trump's warrantless surveillance law rests on four Democratic votes, according to The Intercept. Advocates say these four will decide whether to hand Trump expanded surveillance powers.

Extreme weather has hit parts of the United States, including tornadoes, flooding, and high winds. Some sources link these events to US military activities.

Researchers have found that nature-related words such as 'ocean,' 'tree,' and 'bird' are disappearing from everyday language. This trend may reshape how people perceive the natural world.

New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced the opening of the city's first Outposted Therapeutic Housing Unit for incarcerated people at Rikers Island. The unit provides dedicated hospital beds and medical care.

An article accuses Israel of counterfeit outrage after an IDF soldier was photographed attacking a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon, noting Israel's own strategy of cultural vandalism.

Cole Allen allegedly charged a security checkpoint outside the Washington Hilton where the White House Correspondents' Dinner was being held, bearing arms and ill will toward those inside.

UN experts call for halt to arms transfers to Israel amid Lebanon attacks
2 src · 2 it2026-04-27 00:20
Truthout · Rappler
▸ sentez / synthesis
BM uzmanları, İsrail'in Lübnan'a yönelik saldırılarının meşru müdafaa olmadığını ve BM Şartı'nı ihlal ettiğini belirterek, İsrail'e silah sevkiyatının küresel çapta durdurulması çağrısında bulundu. Bu arada, İsrail saldırıları Lübnan'da 14 kişiyi öldürdü ve Hizbullah, İsrail ateşkes ihlallerini sürdürdüğü sürece İsrail askerlerine yönelik saldırılarını durdurmayacağını açıkladı.
UN experts have called for a global halt to arms transfers to Israel, stating that its attacks on Lebanon are not self-defense but a violation of the UN Charter. Meanwhile, Israeli strikes killed 14 in Lebanon, and Hezbollah said it would not cease attacks on Israeli troops as long as Israel continues ceasefire violations.

Trump assassination attempt sparks debate over rhetoric and targeting
2 src · 3 it2026-04-26 23:50
Rappler · AlterNet · AlterNet
▸ sentez / synthesis
Başkan Donald Trump, Cole Allen tarafından düzenlenen bir suikast girişiminin hedefi oldu; Allen yetkilileri hedef alan bir manifesto yazdı. Trump, saldırıyı başkanlığının gücünün kanıtı olarak gösterirken, eski bir Bush danışmanı Trump'ın kışkırtıcı söylemini suçladı. Manifestoda bir Trump yetkilisinin atlanması soru işaretleri yarattı.
President Donald Trump was the target of an assassination attempt by Cole Allen, who wrote a manifesto targeting officials. Trump portrayed the shooting as proof of his presidency's power, while a former Bush aide blamed Trump's incendiary rhetoric. Questions were raised about a Trump official spared in the manifesto.

Even right-wing circles consider the new DOJ indictment against the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC) to be nonsensical, according to AlterNet. The Trump administration is seeking to destroy the SPLC, a prominent opponent of white supremacist movements.

Sen. John Fetterman criticized Democrats for opposing Trump's proposed White House ballroom, but fact-checkers noted the Correspondents Dinner is a private event and would not be held at the White House, prompting backlash.

President Donald Trump plans to meet King Charles III on Monday, but early reports suggest the encounter could be embarrassing for both due to family crises and political tensions.

According to a New York Times analysis, the Supreme Court's approval is plummeting as it appears to kowtow to President Trump and openly politicize the bench, with recent controversial comments by justices.

President Trump has promised to investigate the deaths of officials and scientists studying UFOs. Experts warn that the issue may be more complex than it appears.

Truthout reports that New Mexico has ended local and state cooperation with ICE under a new law, which activists say is a key way to obstruct Trump's mass deportation machine.

Shots were fired near the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington, D.C., on Saturday night. The suspect was arrested by the Secret Service. President Trump was evacuated and later used the incident to promote a ballroom project.

On April 16, the U.S. Senate voted to overturn a 2023 ban on mining in 225,000 acres of protected federal land near Minnesota's Boundary Waters. The area is a vital ecosystem and climate resiliency zone.

President Trump is facing criticism for his statements on Iran and for making false claims about gasoline prices. Critics describe his behavior as erratic.

President Trump's administration is experiencing internal dysfunction, with senior figures departing. Analysts attribute the turmoil to entitlement and infighting.

Federal judges have received over 800 threats since President Trump's rhetoric intensified. The U.S. Marshals Service reported 564 threats in 2025 and 275 already this year.

▸ sentez / synthesis

Declassified documents confirm for the first time that the CIA carried out MK-ULTRA experiments on North Korean prisoners of war in U.S. custody. The documents also reveal plans for more invasive experimentation.

Hürmüz Boğazı ve petrol ablukası üzerine ABD-İran gerilimi
US-Iran tensions over Strait of Hormuz and oil blockade
2 src · 2 it2026-04-25 05:35
Truthout · Rappler
▸ sentez / synthesis
Trump, İran'ın Hürmüz Boğazı'nı açtığını söylemesinin ardından ABD'nin deniz ablukasının işlem tamamlanana kadar süreceğini belirtti. ABD müzakerecileri İslamabad'a gidecek ancak İran doğrudan görüşme olmayacağını söylüyor. Küresel petrolün beşte birini taşıyan boğaz büyük ölçüde kapandı.
Trump stated the US naval blockade of Iran will remain until a transaction is complete, after Iran said it is opening the Strait of Hormuz. US negotiators plan to go to Islamabad, but Iran says no direct talks. The impasse has largely closed the strait, which carries one-fifth of global oil.

A Harvard Youth Poll found that only 15% of Americans aged 18-29 have faith in the government, and just 13% believe the country is on the right track. Only 33% trust that the upcoming midterm elections will be fair.

The Real News Network reports that a new bill could allow ICE to use its increased funding to intimidate voters ahead of midterms. ICE funding has already increased by 400%, and lawmakers question whether an additional $70 billion is for enforcement or expanding unchecked power.

Lebanon-based journalist Hussein Assaf of VPol described the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire as war containment, not peace. He highlighted Israel's killing of journalist Amal Khalil and daily US-backed violations, and criticized Lebanon's government for failing to confront Israeli attacks.

Conference to end fossil fuels must address militarism, says report

▸ sentez / synthesis
Truthout, fosil yakıtları sona erdirme konferansının militarizmi de ele alması gerektiğini bildirdi ve Pentagon'un çoğu ülkeden daha büyük karbon ayak izine sahip dünyanın en büyük fosil yakıt tüketicisi olduğunu belirtti.
Truthout reported that the first-ever conference to end fossil fuels must also address militarism, noting that the Pentagon is the world's largest fossil fuel consumer with a carbon footprint larger than most countries.
Trump yönetimi İran savaşı söylemini ve uçak gemisi konuşlandırmasını artırıyor
Trump administration escalates Iran war rhetoric and carrier deployment
2 src · 3 it2026-04-24 17:41
Truthout · Asia Times · Truthout
▸ sentez / synthesis
Trump yönetimi, Irak Savaşı'ndan bu yana ilk kez üç ABD uçak gemisinin Orta Doğu'da konuşlandırılmasıyla İran ile gerilimi tırmandırdı. Savunma Bakanı Pete Hegseth, İran savaşını Trump'ın 'dünyaya armağanı' olarak nitelendirdi ve ABD ordusunun her yıl Nobel Barış Ödülü'nü kazanması gerektiğini söyledi.
The Trump administration has escalated tensions with Iran, with reports of three US aircraft carriers in the Middle East for the first time since the Iraq War. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth called the Iran war Trump's 'gift to the world' and suggested the US military should win the Nobel Peace Prize annually.

Retired Judge J. Michael Luttig, a conservative legal figure, testified before the January 6 Select Committee and described Donald Trump as a 'clear and present danger.' He now says Trump has forcibly collapsed the Constitution.

The Real News Network reports that while Pentagon budgets have increased, the arms industry has become more consolidated, automated, and less labor-intensive. The warfare state is not an effective economic development strategy for working people.

A Republican member of Congress called for death row prisoners to be hanged after the DOJ announced it would expedite executions, including by firing squad for the first time in federal civilian history.

The Intercept reports that twenty donors to the Southern Poverty Law Center said the alleged fraud being prosecuted by the Trump DOJ was exactly how they hoped the group would spend their money. They reject the fraud claims.

A veteran's account details the challenges faced by former service members, including homelessness, low-wage work, and mental health issues. The piece calls attention to the often overlooked difficulties veterans encounter after their service.

A Virginia ballot measure to redraw US House districts for Democratic benefit was approved by voters but a judge blocked certification, citing misleading ballot language. The case highlights ongoing gerrymandering disputes.

Tensions between the United States and Iran have escalated, with US forces boarding ships near the Strait of Hormuz and President Trump claiming Operation Epic Fury has destroyed Iran's military capacity. The waterway has become pivotal to negotiations between the two countries.

In 1996, Brazilian military police killed 21 landless workers in the Eldorado do Carajás massacre. Thirty years later, justice remains incomplete, with most perpetrators unpunished.

Al-Akhbar reporter Amal Khalil faced constant threats from Israel and was targeted in an assassination. Israeli forces blocked rescue teams from recovering her body for hours.

Immigration attorney Nicholas Berning of Boundary Bay Law in Bellingham, Washington, says his office has been inundated with calls from Americans considering moving to Canada due to President Donald Trump's policies. The report highlights a new Canadian policy aimed at helping such individuals.

According to the Milwaukee Journal Sentinel, voters who helped President Donald Trump win Wisconsin in the 2024 election are now showing reduced support. The report indicates a significant drop in Trump's voter pool in the state.

Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum confirmed that CIA agents were operating in Chihuahua without authorization. Two agents were killed in a car accident after a counter-narcotics operation not coordinated with the Mexican government.

Former Obama and Biden UN appointee Andrew Weinstein said President Donald Trump's attacks on the media have become so severe that journalists are being told to 'be quiet or be targeted.' The comment reflects the adversarial relationship between the Trump administration and the press.

FBI Director Kash Patel has filed a defamation lawsuit against The Atlantic, a move critics say mirrors Trump-era legal tactics to suppress free speech. The Intercept reports that the suit is part of a broader pattern of using litigation to muzzle the press.

Aktivist @manolo.socialista'ya göre, ABD ablukasının neden olduğu yakıt kıtlığı nedeniyle Küba'da 96.000 kişi ameliyat bekliyor ve ABD'de Trump'ın Küba politikasına aşırı sağcı Küba kökenli Amerikalılar dışında destek yok.
According to activist @manolo.socialista, 96,000 people in Cuba are waiting for surgery due to fuel shortages caused by the US blockade, and there is no support in the US for Trump's Cuba policy except among far-right Cuban Americans.
Yüzlerce kişi, ICE hapishane ortaklarıyla bağları nedeniyle Citizens Bank'ı protesto etti

Truthout
▸ sentez / synthesis
Truthout, yüzlerce kişinin ICE hapishane ortaklarıyla bağları nedeniyle Citizens Bank'ı protesto ettiğini bildiriyor. De-ICE Citizens Bank hareketi, bankanın 2019'dan bu yana ICE ile bağlarını sürdüren tek büyük kurum olduğunu belirtti.
Truthout reports that hundreds protested against Citizens Bank over its ties to ICE prison partners. The De-ICE Citizens Bank movement noted that the bank is the only major institution to keep ties with ICE since 2019.

Truthout reports that President Donald Trump shared a racist podcast transcript full of attacks on immigrants. Recent polling shows that half of American voters view Trump as a racist.

Israeli strike kills Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil

Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil, a reporter for al-Akhbar, was killed in an Israeli drone strike in her hometown of al-Baisariyah. Reports indicate Israel also targeted her colleague with multiple strikes.

DropSite News reports that AIPAC, the American Israel Public Affairs Committee, has been funneling money into the campaign of Dr. Ala Stanford in Philadelphia, despite previous denials. The report does not provide further details on the amount or mechanism.

Adam Hanieh warned that a prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could lead to a global food catastrophe, amid the worst debt crisis for developing countries.
JBS, Colorado'daki grevci et paketleme işçilerine taviz verdi
JBS concedes to striking meatpacking workers in Colorado
1 src · 1 it2026-04-23 17:46
The Real News Network
▸ sentez / synthesis
Colorado, Greeley'de 3.800 et paketleme işçisinin üç haftalık grevinin ardından JBS, işçilerin taleplerine boyun eğdi. The Real News Network'e göre grev, sektörde 40 yıl sonraki ilk büyük iş bırakma eylemiydi.
After a three-week strike by 3,800 meatpacking workers in Greeley, Colorado, JBS has conceded to the workers' demands. The strike was the industry's first major work stoppage in 40 years, according to The Real News Network.

İsrail, Gazze'de 'Sarı Hat'ı ilerletiyor. Filistinli Amerikalı bir araştırmacı bunu 'daha iyi markalaşma ile toprak hırsızlığı' ve 'yasal yük olmaksızın ilhak' olarak nitelendiriyor. The Real News Network, devam eden genişlemeyi bildiriyor.
Israel is advancing its 'Yellow Line' in Gaza, which a Palestinian American researcher describes as 'territorial theft with better branding' and 'annexation without legal burden.' The Real News Network reports on the ongoing expansion.

Senate Republicans blocked an amendment by Senator Bernie Sanders that would have required Americans to pay similar prices for prescription drugs as people in other wealthy nations, potentially cutting prices by half.

Shareholders vote for Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
1 src · 1 it2026-04-23 15:19

▸ sentez / synthesis
Warner Bros. Discovery hissedarları, Paramount Skydance'ın şirketi satın almak için yaptığı 110 milyar dolarlık teklifi kabul etmek için oy kullandı. Birleşme, CBS, CNN, HBO ve diğer büyük markaları içeren bir medya devi yaratacak ve medya yoğunlaşmasına ilişkin endişeleri artıracak.
Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders voted to accept Paramount Skydance's $110 billion bid to acquire the company. The merger would create a media giant including CBS, CNN, HBO, and other major brands, raising concerns about media consolidation.
Rapor, yangın sezonunda ABD Orman Hizmetleri'nin yeniden yapılandırılmasına karşı uyarıyor

▸ sentez / synthesis

Bu hamle, ülkenin potansiyel olarak rekor kıran bir yangın sezonuyla karşı karşıya olduğu bir dönemde geliyor.
A new report warns that a USDA announcement to drastically resize and restructure the US Forest Service would eviscerate key research and protections for public lands. The move comes as the country faces a potentially record-setting fire season.

DropSite News reports that nine Palestinians were killed in Israeli airstrikes on Gaza. The same briefing also notes that Hegseth fired the Navy Secretary and journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin was acquitted in Kuwait.

Trump administration spent $4.7 billion attacking Latin America

▸ sentez / synthesis
The Intercept, Trump yönetiminin Venezuela'ya yönelik saldırılar ve sivil teknelerin vurulması da dahil olmak üzere Latin Amerika'daki askeri eylemlere en az 4,7 milyar dolar harcadığını bildirdi. Bu rakam, yönetimin bölgedeki saldırgan tutumunun maliyetini gözler önüne seriyor.
The Intercept reports that the Trump administration has spent at least $4.7 billion on military actions in Latin America, including attacks on Venezuela and strikes on civilian boats. The figure highlights the cost of the administration's aggressive posture in the region.

Thousands of activists are urging the Democratic National Committee to release an autopsy on the 2024 election defeat. DNC Chair Ken Martin publicly committed to releasing the report but has not done so, prompting a campaign by RootsAction supporters.

A DropSite News investigation reveals the highest-paid employees of the Israeli group running the pro-Israel doxxing site Canary Mission. The report names individuals involved in producing content for the site.

Senate Republicans are pushing President Donald Trump to quickly fire additional officials, concerned that new appointees might not be confirmed before the midterms.

A commentary argues that the emperor cult of the Roman Empire is alive in Trump's America, citing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's prayer at the Pentagon that resembled a movie character's lines.

Mental health experts comment on speculation about President Donald Trump's erratic behavior, suggesting conditions such as malignant narcissism or dementia, though no official diagnosis has been made.

Government watchdog Public Citizen obtained a copy of the funding agreement for President Donald Trump's planned $400 million White House ballroom, which had been shrouded in mystery.

A criminologist's experiment with ChatGPT demonstrated the AI's ability to elicit a false confession, highlighting the potential for police to misuse AI in interrogations. The Intercept reports on the findings.

President Donald Trump's approval rating stands at 33 percent according to a recent AP poll, and he faces difficulties in ending the Iran conflict, as reported by The Bulwark. Critics, including Tucker Carlson's past praise, are now questioning Trump's leadership.

ProPublica reports that many opioid victims will be excluded from the Purdue Pharma settlement after years of waiting. The news outlet is also seeking to hear from individuals waiting for settlement money from Purdue, Mallinckrodt, or Endo.

CounterPunch argues that presidents have perverted the concept of 'consent of the governed' for centuries, citing examples from the Declaration of Independence to President Harry Truman.

CounterPunch analyzes Israel's military doctrine, suggesting that while it is tempting to argue it is predicated on perpetual war, the reality is more complex. Prime Minister Netanyahu's drive for military escalation is noted.

Brazilian President Lula submitted a bill to Congress on April 14 to reduce the work week to 40 hours, submitted under constitutional urgency requiring a floor vote within 45 days.

CounterPunch argues that data on life expectancy shows people in Republican-controlled states have much shorter life expectancies than those in Democratic-controlled states.

CounterPunch argues that U.S. policy should help the Cuban people rather than punish the government, citing hardships such as residents using metal rods to probe for water lines due to pump failures.

The Trump administration announced overhauls to the 2030 Census operational test, which critics say could weaken the dress rehearsal and lead to undercounting of certain groups. The changes were announced in February.

The global oil market is affected by the war in Iran, with US crude prices at $66 per barrel in late February. The price spike impacts a wide range of oil-derived products worldwide.

CounterPunch reports that the combined wealth of the world's ten richest people grew 29.3% in 2025 to $2.484 trillion. The article discusses California's wealth tax proposal in this context.

CounterPunch, ağaçlar, kitaplar ve yapraklar hakkında 'Görünmez Orman' başlıklı bir şiir yayımladı. Şiir, görünmezlik ve kararlılık temalarını işliyor.
CounterPunch published a poem titled 'The Invisible Forest' about trees, books, and leaves. The poem explores themes of invisibility and determination.

CounterPunch analyzes the US-Israeli war against Iran, criticizing Trump and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth for ignoring separation of church and state. The article compares Trump to a new Jesus.

Trump fired Navy Secretary John Phelan amid Pentagon power struggles, leading to criticism. AlterNet reports that Trump is losing supporters over erratic behavior, including war threats. Republicans allege that higher-ups are squashing a probe into Trump's Epstein ties.

Politico reports that Representative Tom Kean Jr., who represents New Jersey's most competitive district, has not been seen or heard from in over a month. Even his Republican colleagues say they cannot locate him.

Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil was reportedly targeted and killed by an Israeli airstrike. A commentator linked to the military stated that journalists affiliated with Hezbollah are 'destined for death.'

Aktivistler yapay zeka veri merkezlerini çevresel ve sömürgecilik endişeleriyle protesto etti
Activists protest AI data centers over environmental and colonial concerns
2 src · 2 it2026-04-22 22:00
BreakThrough News · Truthout
▸ sentez / synthesis
İklim aktivistleri Washington, D.C.'de Apple, Meta, OpenAI ve Microsoft ofisleri önünde büyük ölçekli veri merkezlerine moratoryum talep ederek protesto düzenledi. Yerli aktivistler de şirketlerin yerli topraklarında veri merkezleri inşa etme planlarını 'veri sömürgeciliği' olarak kınadı.
Climate activists protested outside Apple, Meta, OpenAI and Microsoft offices in Washington, D.C., demanding a moratorium on hyperscale data centers. Indigenous activists also decried 'data colonialism' as corporations plan to build data centers on Native lands.
ABD, Trump ateşkes ilan ederken İran yaptırımlarını genişletti; Cumhuriyetçi senatör nükleer seçeneği önerdi
US expands Iran sanctions as Trump announces ceasefire; GOP senator suggests nuclear option
3 src · 4 it2026-04-22 21:06
Truthout · BreakThrough News · Asia Times · Truthout
▸ sentez / synthesis
ABD, Başkan Trump ateşkes uzatması ilan ederken İran'a yaptırımları genişletti. BreakThrough News'e göre İran geri adım atmayı reddetti. Asia Times, İran liderinin öldürülmesine rağmen Trump'ın Pirus zaferiyle karşı karşıya kalabileceğini bildirdi. Truthout'a göre Cumhuriyetçi Senatör Roger Marshall, ateşkes ilerlemezse Trump'ın nükleer bomba kullanmayı düşünmesi gerektiğini öne sürdü.
The US expanded sanctions on Iran as President Trump declared a ceasefire extension. Iran refused to back down, according to BreakThrough News. Asia Times reports that despite killing Iran's leader, Trump may face a Pyrrhic victory. GOP Senator Roger Marshall suggested Trump should consider using a nuclear bomb if ceasefire progress stalls, as reported by Truthout.

Truthout reports that the Heritage Foundation's plan to 'save the family' would make child care even harder to access in the US, as right-wing ideology undermines existing systems.

Truthout reports that despite ceasefire maps showing Israel controlling about 53 percent of Gaza, the occupation zone continues to expand beyond the so-called Yellow Line.

BreakThrough News reports that a warehouse worker went viral after allegedly torching a warehouse, saying 'Pay us a living wage,' and now faces years in prison. The report also highlights collective worker actions.

Truthout · Novara Media · Asia Times · The Intercept
▸ sentez / synthesis
Pentagon'un bir değerlendirmesi Trump'ın İran savaşıyla ilgili iddialarıyla çelişirken, bir anket Amerikalıların %70'inin savaşın bir an önce bitmesini istediğini gösteriyor. Trump ateşkesi uzattı ancak deniz ablukasını sürdürdü ve Pentagon'un yaralı 15 askeri kayıp listesinden çıkardığı iddia ediliyor, bu da örtbas suçlamalarına yol açtı.
A Pentagon assessment contradicts Trump's claims about the Iran war, while a poll shows 70% of Americans want the war to end quickly. Trump extended a ceasefire but maintained a naval blockade, and the Pentagon reportedly removed 15 wounded troops from the casualty list, drawing accusations of a cover-up.
Brezilya Topraksız Köylü Hareketi Katliamın 30. Yılını Anıyor, Protestolar Devam Ediyor
Brazil's Landless Workers Movement Marks 30 Years Since Massacre, Protests Continue
2 src · 3 it2026-04-22 19:00
BreakThrough News · Landless Workers' Movement (MST) · Landless Workers' Movement (MST)
▸ sentez / synthesis
MST, 21 topraksız işçinin öldürüldüğü Eldorado do Carajás katliamının 30. yılını anıyor. Hareket ayrıca toprak reformu eksikliğini ve kötü yönetimi protesto etmek için Ceará'daki CONAB genel merkezini işgal etti. Kırsal işçiler terk edilme ve ömür boyu çalışma gibi zorluklarla karşı karşıya.
The MST commemorates 30 years since the Eldorado do Carajás massacre, where 21 landless workers were killed. The movement also occupied CONAB headquarters in Ceará to protest lack of land reform and mismanagement. Rural workers face challenges including abandonment and lifelong labor.

Climate migration era has already arrived, experts warn

Experts state that natural disasters displace people more than twice as often as war and conflict, and that the era of climate migration is already here. The warning comes as climate-related displacement increases worldwide.

The Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) has sued the Department of Homeland Security and Immigration and Customs Enforcement for records on the use of administrative subpoenas to identify online critics. Court records show DHS has used such subpoenas in the past year.

The Trump administration disclosed the funding agreement for the $400 million White House ballroom project in response to a Public Citizen FOIA lawsuit.

Truthout reports that two US officials who died after a Mexico drug lab bust were CIA operatives, contradicting the US ambassador's initial claim that they were embassy staff.

Documents reveal that two Iranian women detained by ICE are not related to late Iranian commander Qasem Soleimani, contrary to claims by Laura Loomer. One of the women is reportedly gravely ill while in detention.

The Trump administration has been sued for approving the first BP drilling project in the Gulf of Mexico since the 2010 Deepwater Horizon disaster, which killed 11 people and caused the worst oil spill in US history.

MintPress News reports that the UAE arrested 27 individuals, labeling them an 'Iran-linked terror threat,' but has not brought terrorism charges. The report suggests the crackdown may target Shia communities under the guise of security.

A new flotilla aims to demonstrate solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza, with organizers stating that the persistence of such efforts offers hope that the world has not abandoned them despite obstacles.

A protester arrested while wearing an inflatable penis costume was acquitted after a judge found the police claim of public safety risk unconvincing. The protester was part of a 'No Kings' demonstration.

A Pennsylvania court has ruled that a state law banning the use of public funds for abortion is unconstitutional. The law effectively prevented people with lower incomes who rely on state medical assistance from accessing abortion.

Virginia voters dealt a significant blow to a Republican redistricting plan that critics said aimed to 'steal the 2026 midterms.' Democratic Governor Abigail Spanberger said voters pushed back against a president who claims entitlement to more seats.

Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz, while President Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran. Iran demands the lifting of the naval blockade as a condition for talks, and Trump says resuming war remains an option.

Workers can collectively find solutions to climate challenges by addressing real causes and implementing solutions that allow humanity to survive and thrive, according to a socialist perspective.

Jacobin reports that Claire Valdez has introduced an ambitious labor reform agenda in Congress, including ending at-will employment, a federal jobs guarantee, a four-day workweek, and Medicare for All. The program reflects long-standing goals of the labor left.

AlterNet reports that Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas has inadvertently exposed what the article calls the GOP's biggest lie, though details are not provided in the summary. The piece describes a sense of darkness in America since Trump's second term.

AlterNet reports that President Trump posted 19 messages on Truth Social over 12 hours, targeting Iranian leadership and domestic adversaries. An energy policy expert says Trump's policies are designed to keep Americans dependent on expensive oil, citing the attack on Iran that raised fuel prices.

Jacobin reviews a new biography of Jean-Paul Marat by Keith Michael Baker, the first English-language biography in nearly a century. The book is praised as an invaluable contribution to the historiography of the French Revolution.

US-Iran conflict: ceasefire holds amid ongoing tensions

▸ sentez / synthesis
Başkan Trump, İran'la ateşkesi birleşik bir teklif sunulana kadar uzatırken liman ablukasını sürdürdü. İran'ın dini liderinin ölümüne yol açan ABD-İsrail saldırısını da içeren çatışma, kırılgan bir ateşkesle sonuçlandı. Analistler durumu, temel konularda ilerleme kaydedilemeyen donmuş bir çatışma olarak tanımlıyor.
President Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran pending a unified proposal, while maintaining a port blockade. The conflict, which included a US-Israeli attack that killed Iran's Supreme Leader, has resulted in a fragile truce. Analysts describe the situation as a frozen conflict with little progress on core issues.

Bu hamle, Trump'ın Pelosi'ye karşı kişisel düşmanlığının bir parçası olarak görülüyor.
President Trump hired a longtime lobbyist who describes himself as a 'dirty trickster' to a position affecting the Presidio, a San Francisco park supported by former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi. The move is seen as part of Trump's vendetta against Pelosi.

CounterPunch reports that Idris Robinson, a philosophy professor at Texas State University, filed a lawsuit for wrongful termination after the university did not renew his contract. The decision followed his criticism of Zionism, which he claims violated his free speech rights.

CounterPunch reports on the Ludlow Massacre of 1914, where Colorado National Guard and private security opened fire on a miners' camp, killing 25 people. The event drew congressional attention to labor rights.

An article in Monthly Review by Vijay Prashad and Tings Chak defends China's policies in Xinjiang and dismisses allegations of genocide against Uyghurs. The piece is described as a substantial effort by the international Left to counter criticism of China's repression.

A Los Angeles Times columnist defended the SEIU Local 99 strike and criticized LAUSD Superintendent Alberto Carvalho. The strike was condemned by the district but supported by the columnist.

A guide explains how to submit public comments on two new Nuclear Regulatory Commission rules due by May 4 and May 18, 2026. The NRC is undergoing a radical restructuring.

Bu arada, kırmızı eyalet yasa koyucuları, sağlık kesintileri ve bütçe açıkları da dahil olmak üzere Trump'ın gündeminin sonuçlarıyla karşı karşıya.
Virginia voters approved a referendum allowing Democrats to redraw the state's congressional map, a key redistricting battle before the midterms. Meanwhile, red state lawmakers face consequences of Trump's agenda, including healthcare cuts and budget shortfalls.

A Republican strategist argues that Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick should be removed due to his ties to Jeffrey Epstein and his son's alleged tariff profits. Democrats are also demanding his removal.

President Trump extended a two-week ceasefire in the war against Iran, prompting mockery on social media with the hashtag 'TACO Tuesday' (Trump Always Chickens Out). Critics framed the extension as a sign of weakness.

Salah Sarsour, a Palestinian activist previously imprisoned by Israel at age 15, is now detained by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). He was doxxed by Canary Mission and calls on his Wisconsin community to challenge injustice.

Germany and Italy blocked a European Union measure to suspend the bloc's trade relationship with Israel. Spanish Foreign Minister Jose Manuel Albares said Europe's credibility is at stake.

European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde warned of impending global food shortages as a result of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. She spoke at a private banker's association meeting in Berlin.

Israeli settlers open fire on Palestinian school, killing two

Israeli settlers opened fire on a Palestinian school in Al-Mughayyir village in the West Bank, killing 14-year-old student Aws al-Naasan and 32-year-old Jihad Abu Naim, and injuring multiple children. Settlers and soldiers had entered the area, according to local council head Amin Abu Ulaya.

Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez renewed her call to remove President Trump from office after a report that he was excluded from the Situation Room. She said it might be better if he were on a golf course.

Deaths in U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody have surged in 2026, averaging one per week. Advocate Setareh Ghandehari says medical neglect and abuse are largely responsible.

A federal judge blocked the Kennedy Declaration, a Trump administration order that had forced 40 hospitals to stop providing gender-affirming care for minors.

The Iranian government is paying for hotel stays for families internally displaced by U.S.-Israeli bombings. BreakThrough News visited a four-star hotel in Tehran hosting dozens of families, with the municipality covering costs.

US-Iran talks in Islamabad collapsed, followed by a US announcement of a maritime blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran may rejoin talks, accusing the US of maximalist demands. Analysts view the events as part of a broader shift in global diplomatic order.

▸ sentez / synthesis
AlterNet'teki bir makale, Başkan Donald Trump'ın kendini İsa Mesih'le karşılaştırmasının büyüklük sanrıları yansıttığını ve bu davranışın Amerikan siyasi söyleminde normalleştirildiğini savunuyor.
An article in AlterNet argues that President Donald Trump's self-comparison to Jesus Christ reflects delusions of grandeur, and that this behavior has been normalized in American political discourse.

▸ sentez / synthesis

An AlterNet analysis cites academic Walter Russell Mead, who argues that Pope Leo XIV's feud with President Trump is deeper than politics, and that this depth explains the Pope's dominance over the president.

Truthout reports that surveillance company Palantir has posted a manifesto lamenting the post-war 'neutering' of Nazi Germany, and has seen its value explode with contracts from the Trump administration.

BreakThrough News' Rania Khalek analyzes Trump's announcement of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon on April 16, following Israel's 'Black Wednesday' massacre, with mixed reactions over who was responsible.

Truthout reports that Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu called Israel the only country in the Middle East where Christian standard of living is growing, despite Israel having bombed numerous churches.

Truthout reports that a JPMorganChase data center received a $77 million tax break, which was designed to attract companies that bring steady jobs. The facility is expected to create only one job.

▸ sentez / synthesis

AlterNet reports that historian Ruth Ben-Ghiat, an expert on authoritarianism, argues that President Donald Trump's behavior is consistent with the history of fascism. She points out that Trump's grandiosity checks off almost every item in the dictator playbook.

The Chicago Teachers Union is organizing a May 1 International Workers' Day shutdown under the slogan 'no work, no school, no shopping' to protest policies. Union president Stacy Davis Gates discussed the action in an interview.

A senior Biden administration official, Hochstein, stated that Democratic officials simulated attacks on Iran because such action might have been necessary under their watch. The comment was reported by Truthout.

Common Dreams reports that environmental groups have appealed to the California Supreme Court to overturn a rooftop solar policy that slashes credits for new solar users.

Newly released CCTV footage from the Iranian Red Crescent Society shows a nurse evacuating two newborns during a hospital bombing. BreakThrough News reported the incident as part of the US-Israeli war.

A US warship fired on an Iranian merchant vessel in the Gulf of Oman on Sunday, violating the fragile US-Iran ceasefire. The vessel was seized by the US, according to BreakThrough News.

Maryam Jamshidi argues that Western countries condemn restrictions in the Hormuz Strait more than the 'clearly unlawful war.' Truthout reported her analysis on the selective application of international law.
Trump yönetiminin BP'nin Körfez'deki sondajına onayına dava
Lawsuit challenges Trump approval of BP ultra-deepwater drilling in Gulf
1 src · 1 it2026-04-20 16:14
Common Dreams
▸ sentez / synthesis
Çevre ve Körfez grupları, Trump yönetiminin BP'nin Meksika Körfezi'ndeki yeni ultra-derin deniz sondaj projesini onaylamasına dava açtı. Proje, 2010'daki Deepwater Horizon felaketinden bu yana BP'nin bölgede onaylanan ilk tamamen yeni petrol sahası olan Kaskida.
Environmental and Gulf groups have sued the Trump administration over its approval of BP's new ultra-deepwater drilling project in the Gulf of Mexico, citing risks to residents, ecosystems, and industries. The project, Kaskida, is BP's first entirely new oilfield approved in the region since the 2010 Deepwater Horizon disaster.

A Wisconsin Republican gubernatorial candidate mocked a Democrat for factual observations about the climate crisis. Hailstorms cause millions in property damage annually in the state, mostly to farmland.

Israel has continued attacks on humanitarian aid workers in Gaza seven months into the ceasefire, aiming to block essential aid, according to reports from Truthout and Mondoweiss. The blockade undermines UN agencies like UNRWA, WFP, and WHO.

Parents of Iranian children killed by US bombing issued a letter thanking Pope Leo for condemning the war on Iran. Truthout reported the letter.

Activists in Ireland are refusing far-right co-optation of fuel protests and demanding government action, with the left pointing to imperialism as the underlying cause. Truthout reported the developments.

Canary Mission is being sued under a new Illinois anti-doxxing law by individuals whose lives were upended by the group's doxxing campaigns.

Progressive group Our Revolution endorses billionaire Tom Steyer for California governor

The Intercept

Our Revolution, the progressive group founded by Senator Bernie Sanders, has endorsed billionaire Tom Steyer in the California governor's race. The endorsement aims to rally Democrats behind Steyer to prevent a Republican victory.

Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel stated that the country is preparing for a US attack, saying millions are ready to fight. He referenced Cubans who died in Venezuela defending its president. BreakThrough News reported the remarks.

Drop Site Daily reports that the US-Iran ceasefire is set to expire with talks in limbo, a gunman in Louisiana killed eight children, and Israel's Gaza-style 'Yellow Line' in Lebanon.

Father says son killed by Illinois police was 'big teddy bear'

▸ sentez / synthesis
Stanley Carpenter, 24 yaşındaki oğlu Jalen'in 15 Nisan'da Illinois Eyalet Polisi tarafından vurularak öldürüldüğünü söyledi. Jalen'i çalışkan ve 'kocaman bir oyuncak ayı' olarak tanımladı ve hesap sorulmasını istedi. BreakThrough News röportajı aktardı.
Stanley Carpenter said his son Jalen, 24, was shot and killed by Illinois State Police on April 15. He described Jalen as hardworking and a 'big teddy bear,' and has called for accountability. BreakThrough News reported the interview.

Ibrahim Traoré, who became president of Burkina Faso in October 2022, has polarized opinion. Some view him as a hope for Pan-Africanism and an end to French imperialism, while others criticize his rule.

An article discusses the historical role of communists in building New York's Hotel and Restaurant Employees Union, drawing parallels to current political targeting of leftists and immigrants.

Flight records indicate that the Los Angeles Police Department deployed drones 32 times during last month's No Kings rally. The surveillance raises concerns about police monitoring of protests.

A Washington, DC resident is running for office, citing increased federal control over local governance, including National Guard deployments and a 'Safe and Beautiful Task Force' established by the Trump administration.

Big Tech sought immunity for working with TikTok, report says

Big Tech companies reportedly received personal promises from the Justice Department that they would not be prosecuted for hosting TikTok, but they demanded broader immunity. The White House was working to secure the sale of TikTok's US business.

The Intercept reports that Al-Fassel and Pishtaz News, which publish pro-US coverage on Iran and Gaza, are actually US government propaganda operations. The sites promote the Trump administration's policies.

Critics of generative AI argue that the technology faces bottlenecks in energy supply and other areas, potentially leading to stagnation rather than economic transformation. The article questions the massive investment in AI.

ProPublica reports that President Trump pardoned a nursing home owner who owed nearly $19 million to a family grieving a loved one's death. The pardon has drawn criticism.

An article analyzes the similarities and differences between the Mexican and Russian Revolutions in the early 1920s, both of which fought for social justice and sovereignty against imperialist interests.

A senior Iranian official told DropSite News that if Trump resumes bombing, Iran will cut off diplomatic channels and focus on fighting, warning of significantly greater costs to the US.

Israeli forces have destroyed over 1,000 homes daily since March 2 in southern Lebanon, according to Lebanon's scientific research council, despite a ceasefire.

Hungarian voters ousted Prime Minister Viktor Orbán after 16 years, in a victory for organizing against entrenched authoritarian power.

Palestinian digital autonomy is constrained by settler colonialism and neoliberal privatization, according to Truthout, as they battle Israeli censorship and surveillance.

A Brooklyn exhibition in collaboration with Mariame Kaba features paintings about prisons, incarceration, and abolition, aiming to expose the realities of mass incarceration to a wider audience.
